quality.rationale: 需结合度规归一化条件分步构造类时/类空基矢及对偶形式，物理场景对应静止观测者局部惯性系，解答详尽规范。

origin.question:
Problem 16.1. Find basis vectors (and a dual basis of 1-forms) for orthonormal tetrads in a spherical geometry. Take the legs of the tetrad to be in the $\mathbf{t}$ , $\mathbf{r}$ , $	heta$ , and $\phi$ directions of the isotropic coordinate system whose metric is
$$
d s^{2} = - e^{2 \Phi} d t^{2} + e^{2 \mu} (d r^{2} + r^{2} d \Omega^{2}).
$$
origin.answer: $\\mathbf{e}_{\\hat{0}} = e^{-\\Phi} \\frac{\\partial}{\\partial t}$, $\\mathbf{e}_{\\hat{r}} = e^{-\\mu} \\frac{\\partial}{\\partial r}$, $\\mathbf{e}_{\\hat{\\theta}} = e^{-\\mu} r^{-1} \\frac{\\partial}{\\partial \\theta}$, $\\mathbf{e}_{\\hat{\\phi}} = e^{-\\mu} (r \\sin \\theta)^{-1} \\frac{\\partial}{\\partial \\phi}$;\n$\\tilde{\\omega}^{\\hat{0}} = e^{\\Phi} dt$, $\\tilde{\\omega}^{\\hat{r}} = e^{\\mu} dr$, $\\tilde{\\omega}^{\\hat{\\theta}} = e^{\\mu} r d\\theta$, $\\tilde{\\omega}^{\\hat{\\phi}} = e^{\\mu} r \\sin\\theta d\\phi$
origin.solution:
## Solution

Solution 16.1. Since an observer's time axis points along his 4-velocity, if he is stationary in the $(t,r,	heta,\phi)$ coordinate system then his time axis must be along $\partial/\partial t$ (and normalized properly). Accordingly, take $\mathbf{e}_{\hat{\mathbf{o}}} = \mathbf{f}\partial/\partial t$ , where $\mathbf{f}$ is to be determined by normalization:
$$
- \mathbf {1} = \mathbf {e}_{\hat { oldsymbol {0}}} \cdot \mathbf {e}_{\hat { oldsymbol {0}}} = \mathbf {f}^{2}  rac {\partial}{\partial t} \cdot  rac {\partial}{\partial t} = \mathbf {f}^{2} \mathbf {g}_{0 0} = - \mathbf {f}^{2} \mathbf {e}^{2 \hat { oldsymbol {\Phi}}}.
$$
Thus $\mathbf{f} = \mathbf{e}^{-\Phi}$ and
$$
\mathbf {e}_{\hat {0}} = \mathbf {e}^{- \Phi}  rac {\partial}{\partial t}. 	ag {1}
$$
The three directions, $\partial/\partial	heta$ , $\partial/\partial\phi$ , $\partial/\partial\mathbf{r}$ are all orthogonal to $\partial/\partial t$ and choosing basis vectors along them, with unit normalization (same technique as above) yields
$$
\mathbf {e}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} = \mathrm {e}^{- \mu}  rac {\partial}{\partial \mathrm {r}}, \quad \mathbf {e}_{\hat {	heta}} = \mathrm {e}^{- \mu} \mathrm {r}^{- 1}  rac {\partial}{\partial 	heta}, \quad \mathbf {e}_{\hat {\phi}} = \mathrm {e}^{- \mu} (\mathrm {r} \sin 	heta)^{- 1}  rac {\partial}{\partial \phi}. 	ag {2}
$$
The basis vectors in Equations (1) and (2) constitute an orthonormal proper reference frame for a stationary observer. Dual 1-forms, $	ilde{\omega}^{\hat{a}}$ , are found by the requirement
$$
< 	ilde {\omega}^{\hat {a}} , e_{\hat { eta}} > = \delta^{a}  eta 	ag {3}
$$
and are easily seen to be
$$
	ilde {\omega}^{\hat {0}} = \mathrm {e}^{\phi} \widetilde {\mathrm {d t}} , \quad 	ilde {\omega}^{\hat {\mathrm {r}}} = \mathrm {e}^{\mu} \widetilde {\mathrm {d r}} , \quad 	ilde {\omega}^{\hat {	heta}} = \mathrm {e}^{\mu} \mathrm {r} \widetilde {\mathrm {d} 	heta} , \quad 	ilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} = \mathrm {e}^{\mu} \mathrm {r} \sin 	heta \widetilde {\mathrm {d} \phi} . 	ag {4}
$$
